title: Research

vocabulary.meaning: facts or information collected for study
a set of questions asked to many people
a test done to learn something new
to find the size, amount, or level of something

questions: What comes to mind when you hear the word 'research'?
What would you like to research?
How important is research?
What are a few things you have researched recently?
Would you like to work as a researcher?
What would you like your government to spend more money on researching?
What things do you think humans first started researching?
How many answers are there still to find and how many things still to research?
What are the biggest breakthroughs researchers have made recently?
Does research always lead to positive things?
What is market research? Do you like being surveyed by market researchers?
What are your views on stem cell research?
What do you think a medical researcher does on an average day?
What do you think about using animals for research?
Do you think that some research your government pays for is totally useless?
Have you ever had to write a research paper?
Do you like online research?
A research project found out that teenagers were moody. What do you think of people getting paid to find this out?
What piece of research would you like to do on your class/family/friends today?
Would you volunteer to be a guinea pig in laboratory research of new medicines or medical treatments?
